title: Reflogs, Reachability Windows, and Recovery · Repository Checkpoint
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for reflogs, reachability windows, and recovery and requires the smallest safe reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.
Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G13 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it. Competency probe: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.

Which action demonstrates Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain per-ref and head reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Expiration Retention Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G20 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.
